key_insight: Logic supplies transparent rule-based representations, while optimization turns those rules into efficient inference engines for explainable and trustworthy AI.
engineering_relevance: The paper shows that modern optimization methods can make rule-based AI practical for reproducibility, fairness, and post-hoc explanation in engineering systems.
method: Surveyed logic-optimization hybrids: probabilistic logic, Bayesian logic, belief logics, Dempster-Shafer theory, and default logic.
Uses decision diagrams and logic-based Benders decomposition to compute projections, a core inference problem.
Applies postoptimality analysis and Boolean regression to explain conclusions and infer logical formulas from noisy data.
evidence_signal: The abstract presents a conceptual survey and methodology review rather than reporting a single experimental dataset or benchmark result.
watch_item: Practical deployment may be limited by scalability and modeling complexity when encoding large rule bases or noisy real-world data.
